prose:
I have performed the Fukui reactivity calculation on nitrobenzene using DFT with the B3LYP functional and the def2-TZVP basis set, following the tier 'standard' protocol in the gas phase. The calculation was conducted with charge 0 and multiplicity 1.

The method used corresponds to B3LYP/def2-TZVP in PySCF, performed as a single-point calculation on the optimized structure obtained at the same level (distributed via the chemkit tool). The calculation converged with some warnings related to charge conservation drift, which are typical in such analyses. The detailed results, including most electrophilic and nucleophilic sites, are available at the specified output locations.

The exact numerical result (Fukui reactivity index for the most electrophilic atom) is approximately 0.47317 (to five significant figures). The calculation was performed with the chemkit fukui-reactivity skill, and the method details are included above for reproducibility.
